# SylvenOS Code of Conduct

## Our Mission

SylvenOS exists to Learn, Build, and Educate.

We believe great software is created through collaboration, respect, curiosity, and shared knowledge.

We are committed to providing a welcoming, inclusive, and harassment-free environment for everyone regardless of experience level, identity, background, nationality, or technology preference.

---

## Expected Behavior

Community members are expected to:

- Be respectful and constructive.
- Welcome newcomers and help them grow.
- Share knowledge openly.
- Give and receive feedback professionally.
- Focus discussions on improving the project and community.
- Respect differing viewpoints and experiences.

---

## Unacceptable Behavior

Examples include:

- Harassment or discrimination.
- Personal attacks.
- Trolling or inflammatory comments.
- Intimidation or threats.
- Publishing private information without consent.
- Repeated disruptive behavior.

---

## Enforcement

Project maintainers are responsible for enforcing this Code of Conduct.

Actions may include:

- Warning
- Temporary restriction
- Removal from discussions
- Ban from repositories or community spaces

---

## Reporting Issues

If you experience or witness unacceptable behavior, please contact the maintainers through GitHub Discussions or repository maintainers.

All reports will be reviewed confidentially.

---

## Attribution

Inspired by the Contributor Covenant and adapted for the SylvenOS community.
